steps.title: Track the changed opinion
steps.content: The claim is not just that Vanya dislikes Professor Serebrakoff. It says Vanya's opinion has changed significantly. A supporting quotation therefore needs a before-and-after pattern: evidence of what Vanya used to think about the professor and evidence of what he thinks now.

steps.title: Require Vanya's own view
steps.content: The quotation also needs to come from Vanya or clearly reveal Vanya's perspective. A line from the professor would not prove Vanya's opinion, and a line about Vanya's sacrifices would only show resentment unless it also shows the earlier admiration that changed.

steps.title: Separate complaints from change
steps.content: Choice A shows Vanya resenting years of unpaid or unthanked estate work, but it does not show an earlier positive opinion. Choice B is Professor Serebrakoff speaking about Vanya, so it cannot show Vanya's own changed view. Choice C describes Vanya's sacrifices for the estate and his sister, but again it gives only grievance, not a shift in opinion. Choice D says Vanya was once proud of Serebrakoff and treated his words as inspired; now he calls himself deceived and sees the professor's fame as empty. The answer is D.
